subject: Anthropology
title: Epigenetics and Evolution
description: A micro-lesson that makes Epigenetics and Evolution usable.

hook.text: A famine in one generation changes gene expression in the next. Your grandparents' diet shapes your biology.

buildup.text: Epigenetics studies changes in gene expression that don't alter DNA itself but can be inherited across generations.

discovery.text: Environmental stressors like famine, pollution, and trauma can switch genes on or off in offspring.

twist.text: This means the environment shapes heredity more than we thought. Lamarck wasn't entirely wrong after all.

climax.text: Epigenetics adds a new layer to evolution. Genes aren't just destiny — they respond to the world around them.

punchline.text: Your genes listen to the environment.

quiz.question: What does epigenetics study?
options: Heritable changes in gene expression without DNA alteration
